description: Add production Dockerfiles for every deployable that lacks one. Today only apps/quantmail, services/ws-gateway, and infra/docker/Dockerfile.secure exist. The other 12 app backends and 7 services have no container build. Use the existing quantmail + ws-gateway Dockerfiles as multi-stage pnpm+turbo templates (Node 22).
steps: For each app with a Fastify backend (quantchat, quantai, quantcalendar, quantdocs, quantdrive, quantmeet, quantads, quantsync, quantube, quantneon, quantmax, quantedits), add apps/<app>/Dockerfile that: installs via pnpm with the workspace, builds the app + its deps with `turbo build --filter=<pkg>...`, prunes to a runtime image, and runs the backend entrypoint.
For Next.js apps, produce a standalone Next build (output: 'standalone') and a separate runtime stage for the frontend if served separately from the Fastify backend.

For each service (cdc-relay, ci-runner, git-server, matchmaking, moderation-worker, search-indexer, smtp-inbound), add services/<svc>/Dockerfile following the ws-gateway pattern; CMD runs the service's main/start (depends on Phase 83 FEAT-005 entrypoints for smtp-inbound + ci-runner).
Add .dockerignore entries (node_modules, .turbo, .next, dist test artifacts) per image to keep builds small.
Verify each image builds: `docker build -f apps/<app>/Dockerfile .` (and services). Capture image sizes.
acceptance_criteria: Every app backend and every service has a Dockerfile that builds successfully
Next.js apps build in standalone mode
Images use Node 22 multi-stage builds and prune dev deps
docker build succeeds locally for each new image
